title: TrailGenic Fasted Autophagy Dataset
description: Shell dataset module for TrailGenic physiology adaptation science modeling stimulus, response, and adaptation for fasted autophagy.

records.mechanism: AMPK activation during fasted endurance exercise
physiological_response: upregulated fatty acid transport into mitochondria

supporting_science.finding: Training with low glycogen availability upregulated oxidative enzymes and enhanced endurance-related adaptations.
AMPK functions as a central cellular energy sensor activated by energetic stress such as exercise and low fuel availability.

records.mechanism: mTOR suppression and initiation of autophagy signaling

supporting_science.finding: AMPK and mTOR pathways converge on ULK1 to regulate autophagy initiation under nutrient and energy stress.
Exercise induces autophagy and is linked to improved metabolic regulation through autophagy-related pathways.

records.mechanism: Shift toward fat oxidation during fasted endurance exercise
physiological_response: higher lipolysis and free fatty acid availability
increased rates of whole-body fat oxidation
adaptation_outcomes: reduced dependence on frequent carbohydrate feeding

supporting_science.finding: Submaximal exercise intensities are associated with substantial fat oxidation, with intensity-dependent shifts in substrate use.
Regular fasted-state training promoted favorable adaptations in glucose tolerance and oxidative metabolism.

records.mechanism: Mitochondrial biogenesis signaling with fasted training
physiological_response: enhanced transcription of oxidative metabolism genes
adaptation_outcomes: higher fatigue resistance during prolonged exercise

supporting_science.finding: Endurance exercise activates molecular signals linked to mitochondrial biogenesis and oxidative adaptation.
Low-glycogen/fasted-like training context increased markers of mitochondrial adaptation in trained muscle.

records.mechanism: Development of metabolic flexibility through repeated fasted aerobic exposure

stimulus.exercise_type: periodized fasted and fed endurance sessions
stimulus.exercise_intensity: primarily zone 2 with occasional tempo efforts

physiological_response: improved switching between lipid and carbohydrate oxidation
stabilized substrate use across variable fuel availability
adaptation_outcomes: more robust endurance performance across feeding states

supporting_science.finding: Metabolic flexibility is a key determinant of cardiometabolic health and is improved by exercise training.
Fasted exercise increases reliance on fat as fuel and may support training adaptations relevant to metabolic flexibility.
